name: Salad-in-a-Boat
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 2-quart pan, bring water, butter, and salt to a boil.
2. Remove pan from heat and add flour all at once. Beat until well blended.
3. Return pan to medium heat and stir rapidly for 1 minute or until a ball forms in the middle of the pan and a film forms on the bottom of the pan.
4. Remove pan from heat and beat in eggs, one at a time, until the mixture is smooth and glossy.
5. Add cheese and beat until well mixed.
6. Spoon into a greased 9-inch round pan with removable bottom or spring-release sides. Spread evenly over the bottom and up the sides of the pan.
7. Bake crust in a 400 degree oven for 40 minutes or until puffed and brown; turn off the heat.
8. With a wooden pick, prick the crust in 10 to 12 places; leave in the closed oven for about 10 minutes to dry.
9. Remove pan from the oven and cool completely.
10. Prepare egg-vegetable salad: In a bowl, stir all ingredients except eggs together as listed. Coarsely chop the 6 hard-cooked eggs; gently fold into vegetable mixture.
11. Line bottom and sides of boat with spinach leaves. Cut each tomato in half. Pile egg salad over spinach and garnish with cherry tomatoes.
12. Cut boat in thick wedges.
Per Serving (including salad): 18 grams protein, 19 grams carbohydrate, 436 milligrams cholesterol, 483 calories.
